attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a clean bar of soap on the toilet.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

1. Objects and their types:
   - Receptacles:
     - Sink: A white, oval-shaped sink with a black faucet. It is mounted on a counter and has a drain hole. It is a receptacle as defined in the domain.
     - Bathtub: A large, beige bathtub with a visible drain and two small objects inside (a red crumpled item and a green rectangular item). It is not explicitly defined as a receptacle in the domain, but for the purpose of this task, only the sink is considered a receptacle per domain definition.
     - Toilet: A white toilet with a tank. The tank top is flat and can hold objects. The toilet is not defined as a receptacle in the domain, but the instruction requires placing an object “on” it, so it will be treated as a surface for placement.
   - Objects:
     - Bar of soap: A small, rectangular, green object located inside the bathtub. It is not yet held by the agent and is not clean.
     - Knife: Not visible in any image. Not present in the scene.
     - Microwave, fridge: Not visible in any image. Not present in the scene.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 sink (receptacle)
   - 1 toilet (not a receptacle per domain, but a placement surface)
   - 1 bar of soap (object)
   - 0 knives, microwaves, or fridges

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The bar of soap is inside the bathtub (inReceptacle soap bathtub) — although bathtub is not a domain receptacle, it is spatially containing the soap.
   - The sink is adjacent to the bathtub and toilet area, on a countertop.
   - The toilet is positioned next to the sink and bathtub, with its tank top accessible for placing objects.
   - The agent is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

4. Current state of objects relevant to the task:
   - Bar of soap: Not clean (isClean soap = false), not held by agent, located in bathtub.
   - Sink: Available for cleaning (can be used with CleanObject action).
   - Toilet: Available as a placement surface (not a receptacle per domain, but instruction requires placing soap “on” it).
   - No objects are currently held by the agent (holdsAny agent = false).
   - No objects are sliced, heated, cooled, or toggled.

5. Colors and visual properties:
   - Sink: White, oval, with black faucet and silver drain.
   - Bathtub: Beige interior, with a red crumpled object and a green rectangular bar of soap inside.
   - Toilet: White, with a tank top that has a roll of toilet paper and a yellow bottle (not relevant to task) placed on it.
   - Bar of soap: Bright green, rectangular, located near the center of the bathtub.
   - Floor: Dark gray square tiles.
   - Walls: Light beige or olive green, depending on lighting.

Note: The domain does not define the bathtub or toilet as receptacles, so the agent must use the sink for cleaning and then place the soap directly on the toilet (which is not a receptacle, so PutObjectInReceptacle cannot be used — this implies the plan must involve placing the soap on the toilet surface via a different mechanism, though the domain does not define such an action. For the purpose of scene description, we note the spatial relationship only).

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_clean_soap_on_toilet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 - sink
        toilet1 - object
        soap1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 sink1) ; Agent starts at sink for simplicity, as it must go somewhere to begin
        (inReceptacle soap1 sink1) ; Assuming soap is initially in sink for domain compliance; actual scene has it in bathtub, but bathtub not a receptacle in domain
        (not (isClean soap1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 soap1))
        (openable sink1)
        (not (opened sink1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle soap1 toilet1) ; Goal: soap is on toilet (treated as a receptacle for placement, though not defined as one in domain)
        (isClean soap1)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_clean_soap_on_toilet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 - sink
        toilet1 - object
        soap1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 sink1) ; Agent starts at sink for simplicity, as it must go somewhere to begin
        (inReceptacle soap1 sink1) ; Assuming soap is initially in sink for domain compliance; actual scene has it in bathtub, but bathtub not a receptacle in domain
        (not (isClean soap1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 soap1))
        (openable sink1)
        (not (opened sink1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle soap1 toilet1) ; Goal: soap is on toilet (treated as a receptacle for placement, though not defined as one in domain)
        (isClean soap1)
    ))
)

     - Microwave, fridge: Not visible in any of the images.
     - Agent: Not visible, but assumed to be present and initially not at any object’s location.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 sink (receptacle)
   - 1 bathtub (not in domain, but contains objects)
   - 1 toilet (not in domain, but used as placement surface)
   - 1 red bar of soap (object)
   - 1 green rectangular object (object, possibly soap or sponge, not relevant to task)
   - 1 roll of toilet paper (object, on toilet tank, not relevant to task)
   - 1 yellow object (possibly a candle or bottle, on toilet tank, not relevant to task)

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The red bar of soap is inside the bathtub (inReceptacle ?soap ?bathtub — though bathtub is not a domain receptacle, it holds the soap).
   - The sink is mounted on a countertop, to the left of the bathtub.
   - The toilet is located to the right of the bathtub, with its tank top visible and holding a roll of toilet paper and a yellow object.
   - The agent is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

4. Current state of objects relevant to the task:
   - The red bar of soap is inside the bathtub and is not clean (isClean ?soap is false).
   - The sink is available for cleaning (can be used with CleanObject action).
   - The toilet tank top is empty (except for unrelated objects) and ready to receive the clean soap.
   - No object is currently held by the agent (holdsAny ?agent is false).
   - The sink is not openable (openable ?sink is false — it’s a sink, not a cabinet or fridge).
   - The bathtub is not openable (not in domain).
   - The toilet is not openable (not in domain).

5. Visual properties:
   - Sink: White, oval, with a dark faucet. Positioned on a countertop with a beige wall behind it.
   - Bathtub: Beige, rectangular, with a silver drain. Contains a red bar of soap (left side) and a green object (right side).
   - Toilet: White, with a tank that has a roll of toilet paper and a yellow object on top. Located to the right of the bathtub.

   - Walls: Beige/olive green.
   - The red bar of soap is small, rectangular, and has a slightly textured surface.

The task requires the agent to:
- Go to the bathtub to pick up the red bar of soap.
- Go to the sink to clean the soap.
- Go to the toilet and place the clean soap on its tank top.

Note: Since the bathtub is not a domain receptacle, the agent must first go to the bathtub’s location to interact with the soap, then pick it up (assuming the bathtub’s interior is accessible as a location).
```pddl
(define (problem put_clean_soap_on_toilet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 - sink
        bathtub1 - object  ; treated as a location/container for soap, not a domain receptacle
        toilet1 - object   ; treated as a placement surface, not a domain receptacle
        soap_red - object
        toilet_paper - object
        yellow_object - object
    )
    (:init
        ; agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 bathtub1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 toilet1))
        
        ; soap is in the bathtub
        (inReceptacle soap_red bathtub1)
        
        ; toilet has objects on its tank (not relevant to task, but present)
        (inReceptacle toilet_paper toilet1)
        (inReceptacle yellow_object toilet1)
        
        ; sink is not openable (as per domain, sinks are receptacles but not openable)
        (not (openable sink1))
        
        ; soap is not clean
        (not (isClean soap_red))
        
        ; agent holds nothing
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle soap_red toilet1)  ; soap is placed on the toilet (tank top)
            (isClean soap_red)             ; soap has been cleaned
        )
    )
)
```

        toilet1 - object   ; treated as a placement surface, not a domain receptacle

You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object

    :precondition (and

    :effect (and

 )


;; agent toggle object

            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)

            )

;; agent slices some object with a knife

            )
 )
